title: The Battle for the Baratie Escalates
description: On pages 1–4, Krieg’s forces push harder against the Baratie, using numbers, weapons, and intimidation while Sanji and the cooks struggle to hold the line.
scene: The restaurant becomes a battlefield where Krieg’s idea of strength clashes with the Baratie’s pride and the Straw Hats’ refusal to yield.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Well, that was a bit of a nuisance,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: But it's nothing...!

dialogue.speaker: Gin
dialogue.text: We were originally planning on abandoning this ship anyways,

dialogue.speaker: Don Krieg
dialogue.text: So who cares if it's been destroyed.

dialogue.speaker: Don Krieg
dialogue.text: Look at that goofy-looking ship...

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: If we have that boat, we needn't fear even grand line anymore.

dialogue.speaker: Don Krieg
dialogue.text: Having that ship will be more effective,

dialogue.speaker: Gin
dialogue.text: than hanging a white flag or pretending to be the marines.

dialogue.speaker: Don Krieg
dialogue.text: It'll lower our enemies' guards and we'll be able to sneak closer to enemy ships than ever before.

dialogue.speaker: Don Krieg
dialogue.text: It'll be the perfect disguise for us.

dialogue.speaker: Don Krieg
dialogue.text: I see... nobody would ever suspect that there'd be pirates lying in wait,

dialogue.speaker: Don Krieg
dialogue.text: on such a stupid looking ship like that.

dialogue.speaker: Don Krieg
dialogue.text: I could not

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: This era is becoming with pirates on every ocean.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Rebuilding our pirate fleet will be a piece of cake!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: B-But Don...! When we go back to the grand line,

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: What if we were to come across another monster like that "hawk- eyes"...

dialogue.speaker: Don Krieg
dialogue.text: GYAA!

dialogue.speaker: Don Krieg
dialogue.text: You're still afraid of that bastard!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: N-NO...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Do you really think a normal person can just cut down a gigantic gallon!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: What!? What do you mean by that, Don!?

dialogue.speaker: Don Krieg
dialogue.text: Isn't it obvious that he's got some strange power from a devil fruit!?

dialogue.speaker: Don Krieg
dialogue.text: Devil fruit users, who we've only heard of in legends and hearsays...

dialogue.speaker: Don Krieg
dialogue.text: The Grand Line is full of 'em!

dialogue.speaker: Don Krieg
dialogue.text: There's no doubt that the Hawk-eyed man used some strange power when cutting our ship!

dialogue.speaker: Don Krieg
dialogue.text: Even that strawhat-wearing punk there with his weird ability is just another run of the mill pirate in Grand Line!

dialogue.speaker: Zeff
dialogue.text: But "red-legg Zeff,"

dialogue.speaker: Don Krieg
dialogue.text: spent an entire year sailing in Grand Line,

dialogue.speaker: Don Krieg
dialogue.text: Meaning he must've found some method to deal with those monsters!

summary: This part keeps increasing the pressure on the Baratie and exposes the brutality of Krieg’s command structure.
